name: OtpInput
description: One-time password input, auto-advances between boxes.

api.name: OtpInput
api.description: One-time password input, auto-advances between boxes.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the first OTP digit input on first render. Useful for the typical OTP flow where the field appears right after a "code sent" screen and the user should be typing immediately. See Input.AutoFocus for why the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM.

examples.title: 2FA Verification
examples.code:
<Card>
    <CardHeader>
        <Heading Level="3">Two-factor authentication</Heading>
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.</Lumeo.Text>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent>
        <OtpInput @bind-Value="_tfaValue" OnComplete="HandleTfa" />
        @if (_tfaVerified)
        {
            <Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Success">Verified</Badge>
        }
    </CardContent>
    <CardFooter>
        <Button Class="w-full" Disabled="@(_tfaValue.Length < 6)">Verify</Button>
    </CardFooter>
</Card>

examples.title: Variable Length
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_otpLength">
    <SegmentedItem Value="4">4 digits</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="6">6 digits</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="8">8 digits</SegmentedItem>
</Segmented>

<OtpInput Length="@(int.Parse(_otpLength))" Value="@_otpValue" ValueChanged="@(v => _otpValue = v)" />

@if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(_otpValue))
{
    <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Value: @_otpValue</Lumeo.Text>
}

@code {
    private string _otpLength = "6";
    private string _otpValue = "";
}

mdSummary:
# OtpInput

One-time password input, auto-advances between boxes.

**Package:** `Lumeo` · **Category:** Forms/Inputs
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/otp-input

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| OtpInput | FormField | `FormField.FormFieldContext?` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | Length | `int` | `6` |  |
| OtpInput | Value | `string` | `""` |  |
| OtpInput | ValueChanged | `EventCallback<string>` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | OnComplete | `EventCallback<string>` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | InputMode | `string` | `"Numeric"` |  |
| OtpInput | Disabled | `bool` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | Masked | `bool` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | Separator | `string?` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | GroupSizes | `int[]?` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | Required | `bool` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | Invalid | `bool` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | ErrorText | `string?` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | HelperText | `string?` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | Label | `string?` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | Name | `string?` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | AutoFocus | `bool` | `` | Focuses the first OTP digit input on first render. Useful for the typical OTP flow where the field appears right after a "code sent" screen and the user should be typing immediately. See Input.AutoFocus for why the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM. |
| OtpInput | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| OtpInput | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |

## Slots
- (none)

## Events
- `ValueChanged` (`EventCallback<string>`)
- `OnComplete` (`EventCallback<string>`)

## Sub-components
- (none)

## Cascading values it expects
- `FormField` (`FormField.FormFieldContext?`)

## Services it injects
- `IComponentInteropService` ()
- `ILumeoLocalizer` (Lumeo.Services.Localization)

## Keyboard
—

## Example
```razor
<OtpInput />
```

## CSS variables
`--color-destructive`, `--color-input`, `--color-muted`, `--color-muted-foreground`, `--radius`

## Files
- `UI/OtpInput/OtpInput.razor`
